stories.title: Jamesob's guide to running SOTA LLMs locally
stories.summary: A comprehensive guide to running state-of-the-art large language models locally on consumer hardware, sparking major discussion on Hacker News about self-hosted AI.

stories.title: 60% Fable cost cut by converting code to images and having the model OCR it
stories.summary: A creative cost-optimization technique that converts code into images and uses vision models to OCR it back, cutting Fable API costs by 60% through token reduction.

stories.title: Fable 5 Returns Globally After 19-Day Government Suspension
stories.summary: Anthropic's Fable 5 was restored worldwide after the US Commerce Department lifted export controls. Testing proved Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 could reproduce the same jailbreak that triggered the ban.

stories.title: Claude Sonnet 5 Launches at $2/M Tokens, Approaching Opus Performance
stories.summary: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5 at $2/M input tokens with 63.2% agentic coding benchmark scores, 1M token context, and is now the default model for Free and Pro plans.

stories.title: Ask HN: Is anyone experimenting with different ways of using LLMs for coding?
stories.summary: A lively Hacker News discussion exploring novel approaches to leveraging LLMs for software development beyond traditional code completion.

stories.title: White House Advances Voluntary AI Model Standards, Announcement Expected Week of July 7
stories.summary: The White House is finalizing voluntary standards for frontier AI model releases with a 30-day government review window. The framework implements Trump's June 2 executive order on AI security.

stories.title: OpenAI and Broadcom Unveil Jalapeño, First Custom LLM Inference Chip
stories.summary: OpenAI unveiled its first custom inference processor built with Broadcom, a reticle-sized ASIC designed from scratch for LLM workloads. Tape-out took just nine months with deployment slated for late 2026.

stories.title: I Wasn't Allowed Prompting ChatGPT During My Chalk Talk: This Is Discrimination
stories.summary: A provocative essay arguing that prohibiting AI tool usage during academic presentations constitutes discrimination, sparking heated debate about AI in education.

stories.title: AI-Designed Universal Coronavirus Vaccine Passes First Human Trial
stories.summary: University of Cambridge researchers completed the first human trial of a vaccine whose key component was designed entirely by AI. The universal coronavirus vaccine targets all known strains.

stories.title: New Serious Vulnerabilities Spiked Around Release of Claude Mythos Preview
stories.summary: Epoch AI data shows a notable spike in high-severity CVEs coinciding with the release of Claude Mythos Preview, raising questions about AI's role in vulnerability discovery.
